item.title: Apple, Google Crushed California Bill That Helped Smaller Rivals
item.description: Some of the world’s largest technology companies swiftly quashed a California legislative measure that pitted giants like Apple Inc. and Google against smaller rivals, a display of their immense political influence in their home state.

item.title: NBA Push Into Vegas, Seattle Gains Steam With Potential Bidders
item.description: The National Basketball Association has received signs of interest from multiple investor groups for new teams in Las Vegas and Seattle that the league plans to sell for as much as $10 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Truist Loses Trio of Veteran Traders From Municipal Bond Team
item.description: Three members of Truist Financial Corp’s municipal bond department have departed the Charlotte-based bank.

item.title: Sequoia and Nvidia Back Ex-DeepMind Researcher’s New AI Startup at $5.1 Billion Value
item.description: Former Google DeepMind researcher David Silver has raised $1.1 billion in funding for his new artificial intelligence startup Ineffable Intelligence at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Bill Ackman’s Pershing Square IPO Expected to Raise $5 Billion
item.description: Billionaire Bill Ackman’s IPO of his closed-end fund and his alternative asset management company is expected to raise about $5 billion, according to people familiar with the matter, the low end of the targeted fundraising range.

item.title: Data Center Venture Bankrolled by Carlyle Co-Founder Looks to Norway’s Fjords
item.description: Carlyle Group Inc. co-founder Bill Conway is backing a new venture that aims to procure land and power in Norway and sell the assets to data-center developers and technology companies.

item.title: Banks Launch $2 Billion BASF Coatings Loan Sale for Buyout
item.description: Banks have launched a debt sale worth over $2 billion for the auto paint and finishing division of BASF SE, amid a difficult market backdrop for the chemicals industry.

item.title: Colombia Buys Back $4.4 Billion in Bonds Weeks Before Election
item.description: Colombia is buying back $4.4 billion in outstanding bonds, closing out its third such transaction in the last year as it looks to lower borrowing costs weeks before a pivotal presidential election.

item.title: Hut 8 Is Selling Bonds to Help Finance Google-Tied Data Center
item.description: A Hut 8 Corp. project is selling investment-grade bonds on Monday to fund the construction of a data center tied to Alphabet Inc.’s Google, the latest in a string of borrowing by firms spearheading the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Organic Juice Maker Suja, Backer Seek $213 Million in US IPO
item.description: Suja Life Inc., a maker of organic cold-pressed juices and other nutritional beverages, and one of its backers are looking to raise as much as $213.3 million in a US initial public offering.

item.title: Using a Computer to Use My Computer
item.description: AI still needs to reshape our relationship with our computers.

item.title: Why China’s Affordable AI Is a Worry for Silicon Valley
item.description: Chinese AI models are cheaper and more adaptable than the preeminent US platforms, and studies suggest they’re now almost as proficient. How did that happen?

item.title: Stonepeak, Bernhard Said to Near $6 Billion Deal for Cleco Power
item.description: A consortium including Macquarie Group Ltd. is nearing a deal to sell Louisiana utility Cleco Power to Stonepeak Partners LP and Bernhard Capital Partners for nearly $6 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Emerging Stocks an Even Better Bargain After Best Rally in Years
item.description: Emerging-market stocks are rallying three times as fast as US equities this year, and their lead may widen further if relative share valuations are any guide.

item.title: Strategy Slows Bitcoin Buying, Switches Back to Common Sales
item.description: Strategy Inc. bought $255 million of Bitcoin in the previous seven days, slowing the pace of its buying of the digital currency after spending $3.5 billion over the prior two weeks.

item.title: Microsoft to Stop Sharing Revenue With Main AI Partner OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft Corp. will no longer pay revenue to OpenAI and said its partnership with the leading artificial intelligence firm will not be exclusive going forward.

item.title: Nvidia-Tied Data Center Taps Junk-Debt Market for $4.5 Billion
item.description: A data center developer is seeking $4.54 billion in junk-debt financing for an artificial intelligence project tied to Nvidia Corp., testing investor appetite after a recent surge in offerings.

item.title: Walmart Plans to Sell Investment-Grade Bonds on Monday
item.description: Walmart Inc plans to sell dollar-denominated investment-grade bonds in as many as five parts on Monday.

item.title: Redwood Plans $1 Billion Fund for Long, Illiquid Credit Bets
item.description: Redwood Capital Management is seeking to raise about $1 billion for a fund built to hold illiquid credit investments with long time horizons, as complex debt maneuvers stretch out the path to restructurings.

item.title: Swatch Group Investor Battle Heats Up After ISS Backs Activist
item.description: The Swatch Group AG is facing renewed investor pressure after a key proxy adviser Institutional Shareholder Services recommended shareholders support a board candidate backed by a US activist.

item.title: Societe Generale Eyes SRT Deal Tied to Over $10 Billion of Loans
item.description: Societe Generale SA is mulling a significant risk transfer tied to over €9 billion ($10.5 billion) of loans in what would be one of the largest such deals seen in recent years.

item.title: Intel Kicks Off Bond Sale to Help Buy Back Ireland Plant Stake
item.description: Intel Corp. has kicked off the sale of investment-grade debt to help finance a $14.2 billion deal to retake full ownership of an Irish semiconductor plant.

item.title: EBay Rival Vinted Valued at €8 Billion in Share Transaction
item.description: Online retailer Vinted sold shares in a secondary transaction that values the company at €8 billion ($9.4 billion), the company said on Monday.

item.title: Singapore Investigates Cybersecurity Incident Involving Government Contractor
item.description: Singapore’s Land Transport Authority said it has suspended a government construction contractor’s access to its digital system after the company was involved in a cybersecurity incident, CNA reported.

item.title: Private Equity’s Slowdown Hands More Control to Limited Partners
item.description: Investors in private equity funds are gaining more sway over the general partners that manage the vehicles as the industry’s payouts continue to lag the historical average, according to a new report.

item.title: UK Proposes Cutting IPO Timetable by a Week to Boost Listings
item.description: The UK’s market watchdog has proposed cutting timelines for initial public offerings by a week, in an effort to bolster London’s appeal as a hub for new listings.

item.title: Spotify Premium Now Includes Peloton Classes, Marking Push Into Fitness Content
item.description: Spotify Technology SA is now offering workout classes from Peloton Interactive Inc. to premium subscribers, marking the music streaming pioneer’s first major foray into fitness content.

item.title: Qualcomm Soars on Report It’s Working With OpenAI on Smartphone
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. shares jumped in premarket trading on Monday after a closely watched tech industry analyst suggested the chipmaker is working with artificial intelligence giant OpenAI on a smartphone.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 27, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are little changed as of 7:40 a.m. in New York at the start of a week that will feature a raft of megacap tech earnings and central bank decisions.

item.title: Moody’s Upgrades China Outlook to Stable on Economic Resilience
item.description: Moody’s Ratings revised China’s outlook up to stable from negative, citing the country’s sustained growth and debt management, despite lingering domestic and external headwinds.
